title: cicer heterophyllum
intro: Cicer heterophyllum, also known as the bitter vetch or babchi plant, is a perennial herb native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate hair growth and treat skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its antiseptic, antifungal, and antibacterial properties, which help to soothe and heal skin irritations. Cicer heterophyllum is often used in herbal preparations, including oils and extracts, to promote skin health and hair growth.
